what_it_does: This file is a generative design exploration that maps the settled 'mode = the dial' decision onto a {mode × activation-context} matrix, proposing per-mode casts, thought-shapes, grain/budget/brain configurations, and the three non-per-turn activation contexts (background, sense, rollup) for the concurrent-cognition system.
observations: Explicitly marked as a generative draft, not a locked build spec or decision.
Builds a matrix of modes against four activation contexts: per-turn, background, sense, and rollup.
Covers eight modes: listening, walkthrough, text-only, decide-for-me, background, watch-and-react, focus, and off.
Proposes a shared spine of roughly six reusable roles plus mode-unique roles for each mode.
Introduces new thought-shapes: narrate_step_confirm, act_then_report, enrich_one, watch_comment, prepare_surface, and rollup_consolidate.
Provides concrete config tables for part-grain, slot budget, and brain configuration.
Frames brain config as a physical KV-pool tradeoff between 64K deep context and 16K lean swarm breadth on one card.
Includes explicit open calls for Tim, such as the focus mode/role name collision and text-only cast scope.
Emphasizes governance boundaries for decide-for-me actions and the invariant R≈4 reserved floor.
standouts: The {mode × activation-context} matrix as the central organizing device.
The claim that non-staging modes do not reduce cognition but move it into background, sense, and rollup contexts.
The brain-config seesaw tying mode selection to physical GPU KV allocation.
The rollup cast making the cognition system self-instrument and propose its own tuning.
conventions: Follows the locked decision architecture and references file:line anchors.
Uses declarative registry-data style for roles, shapes, and config tables.
Maintains explicit epistemic status and open calls for Tim.
Breaks from a single per-turn cast model by introducing four activation contexts.
concerns: The focus mode versus focus role name collision is acknowledged but left unresolved.
Several proposed roles, shapes, and brain-config assignments are hypothetical and require measurement.
The sense context includes an app_changed event kind that the file notes has no existing repo substrate.
Decide-for-me action routing and adversarial safety claims will need careful implementation.
notes: A generative proposal for how the mode dial selects not just a per-turn cast but a full {mode × activation-context} profile, including idle, sense, and rollup cognition.
questions: Which exact file or table will hold the MODE_GRAIN, MODE_BUDGET, and MODE_BRAIN configurations?
How are sense triggers registered and dispatched relative to the existing event log?
What is the precise schema for a role's modes tuple and its trigger.event and predicate fields?
How does the rollup scheduler decide window size and firing frequency?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to explore what 'mode = the dial' fully unlocks by designing the rest of the matrix; the file does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Mode as a coordinated attention-allocation profile.
Reuse of a small shared role spine across modes.
Cognition distributed across per-turn, idle, sense, and introspective contexts.
Governance and safety boundaries around autonomous action.
Hardware-aware design trading context depth for concurrency breadth.
intention_signals: Extend the locked 'mode = the dial' decision into a complete design vocabulary for concurrent cognition, showing how the dial can reallocate roles, contexts, shapes, grain, budget, and brain config as one coherent profile.
novelty: The explicit two-dimensional {mode × activation-context} matrix and the treatment of background, sense, and rollup as first-class cognition contexts with their own thought-shapes, plus the brain-config seesaw.
contribution: A structured set of proposed casts, roles, shapes, config tables, and activation-context semantics that fill out the mode dial design space while staying within the locked architecture and flagging unresolved decisions.
summary_for_embedding: Generative design exploration proposing a {mode × activation-context} matrix for concurrent cognition, with per-mode casts, shared and mode-unique roles, thought-shapes, grain/budget/brain configs, and background/sense/rollup contexts.
intention_for_embedding: To flesh out the implications of the settled 'mode = the dial' decision so Tim can keep, cut, add, or rename the proposed casts, shapes, and contexts.
